title: Standard outputs should be avoided directly to log anything
description: Standard outputs should not be used directly to log anythingAnalyze your codeadaptability-modularmaintainabilityCode Smellbad-practiceWhy is this an issue?More InfoIn software development, logs serve as a record of events within an application, providing crucial insights for debugging. When logging, it is essential to ensure that the logs are:easily accessibleuniformly formatted for readabilityproperly recordedsecurely logged when dealing with sensitive dataThose requirements are not met if a program directly writes to the standard outputs (e.g., Console). That is why defining and using a dedicated logger is highly recommended.ExceptionsThe rule doesn’t raise an issue for:Console ApplicationsCalls in methods decorated with[Conditional ("DEBUG")]Calls included in DEBUG preprocessor branches
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant: Console.WriteLine("Application started"); // Noncompliant - using Console for logging
compliant:
private readonly ILogger<MyClass> _logger;

_logger.LogInformation("Application started"); // Compliant - using proper logging framework

checks.message: Use a proper logging framework instead of Console.Write/WriteLine.

logic.reasoning: Console output is not suitable for production logging - it lacks formatting, levels, and proper output destinations.
